one_time_description: COVID-19 additional federal funding from ISC ($1,519,052) and FNESC ($103,860)
economic_notes: Two government business enterprises: Penelakut Seafood Ltd. (100% owned, FY Aug 31 2021 revenues $139,978) and Spune'lexutth' Holdings Incorporated (100% owned, FY Mar 31 2022 revenues $769,576). First Nation is economically dependent on ISC funding.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2022 (not 2024). Two GBEs accounted for under modified equity method: Penelakut Seafood Ltd. and Spune'lexutth' Holdings Inc. No schedule of remuneration provided. expenses_salaries_total calculated from Note 11 wages ($4,246,824) plus benefits ($614,684) = $4,861,508. total_government_transfers estimated as ISC + Health Authority + CSETS + Province of BC + FNESC = $16,444,985; OSR = rent + other + recovery = $1,967,857 less recovery $30,147. COVID-19 funding noted as one-time item. annual_debt_service represents net increase in loans payable ($484,601) but actual principal payments were offset by new mortgage advances for housing construction ($789,177). Replacement and operating reserves ($614,107) included in liabilities. No remuneration schedule present in document.
